positions.label: The Moon — Lair of the Father

items.name: Strip Rydia's gear
items.location: Before boarding the boat at Fabul

route.why: Leviathan takes her — unequip everything before sailing

items.notes: Older 2D versions lose departing characters' equipment forever; Pixel Remaster refunds it. Cheap insurance either way.

items.name: Strip Edward's gear
items.location: Before boarding the boat at Fabul

route.why: He washes ashore alone — take his equipment first

items.notes: Same boat, same storm. His Harps aren't worth much, but his armor slots are.

items.name: Strip Tellah's gear
items.location: Tower of Zot — before the confrontation

route.why: The sage does not come back down the tower

items.notes: Unequip him before the top-floor scenes.

items.name: Strip Yang's gear
items.location: Tower of Babil — before the escape

route.why: He stays behind with the cannons

items.notes: His claws travel with him unless you take them first.

items.name: Strip Cid's gear
items.location: The airship escape from the underworld

route.why: He jumps with the bomb right after Yang's farewell

items.notes: Two departures in one beat — check both before the cutscenes chain.

items.name: Strip FuSoYa's gear
items.notes: He returns to the moon's core once the Giant falls.

items.name: Tower of Zot sweep

route.why: The tower vanishes from the world map afterward

items.notes: Open every chest on the climb — there is no second visit.

items.name: Upper Babil sweep

route.why: The cannon floors collapse behind you

items.notes: Everything above the Eblan crossing is one-visit-only.

items.name: Giant of Babil sweep

route.why: The Giant is destroyed when you win

items.notes: The chests inside go down with it.

items.name: Sylph (and the Frying Pan)
items.notes: Find Yang asleep in the Sylvan Cave, fetch the Frying Pan from his wife in Fabul, wake him with it, and return it for the Knife. Rydia learns Sylph at his bedside.

items.name: Asura

route.why: The queen's test — Reflect trivializes her healing

items.notes: Rydia only. The Feymarch hides in the underworld's western cavern.

items.name: Leviathan
items.notes: The king fights only after his queen approves of you.

items.name: Odin
items.notes: The late king's ghost. Strike before he raises his sword — lightning helps.

items.name: Bahamut
items.location: Lair of the Father, the moon

route.why: The Hallowed Father's cave is open the moment you land

items.notes: Count his Mega Flare timer and mirror it with Reflect.

items.name: Rat Tail

route.why: The oddest chest in the underworld starts the Excalibur chain

items.notes: In the Passage of the Eidolons' treasure room.

items.name: Adamantite
items.notes: The tail collector on the northeast isle trades Rat Tail for Adamantite. Reach him with the hovercraft carried by the Big Whale.

items.name: Excalibur

route.why: Hand Kokkol the adamantite and the legendary sword gets reforged

items.notes: Return after a story beat to collect the finished blade for Cecil.

items.name: Lunar Subterrane legendary gear
items.notes: Ragnarok, Murasame, the Masamune, Crystal Mail, the dragon set — each guarded by a chest fight that earns its reputation.

items.name: Pink Tail
items.notes: Flan Princesses in the pink-flan room drop it at roughly 1/64. The grind is the price of the best armor in the game.

items.name: Adamant Armor
items.notes: The tail collector's other trade. Wearable by anyone; wasted on no one.

items.name: Dark Matter
items.notes: Steal from Zeromus itself (GBA/PSP/Pixel Remaster). A trophy stolen from the end of the world.
